skill.name: vision
skill.description: Analyze images using GPT-4o Vision through the verging.ai proxy API, supporting both image URL (JSON) and file upload (multipart) modes.
skill.summary: Analyze images with GPT-4o Vision through the verging.ai API.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell execution and weak cryptography are mostly markdown documentation false positives. The confirmed risk is intended network use: the skill sends image URLs, uploaded files, prompts, and API authorization headers to verging.ai.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Image Data Transfer
medium_findings.description: The network findings are true positives. The skill instructs users to send image URLs, uploaded image files, and prompts to the verging.ai API, which is expected but exposes user content to a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation contains direct API endpoints and curl commands for image URL analysis and file upload. This confirms network transfer of image content, although it matches the skill's stated purpose.

medium_findings.title: Credential-Bearing API Examples
medium_findings.description: The environment and secret-key findings are true positives for credential handling but not evidence of theft. The skill documents API keys in environment variables and Authorization headers, so users must protect keys and avoid exposing commands in logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence shows placeholder API keys and Authorization headers, not hardcoded real secrets. The risk is operational exposure during use rather than malicious credential collection.

low_findings.title: Markdown Shell Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives for runtime command execution. They are fenced documentation examples for npx, export, and curl, and there is no script file that automatically runs them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands appear only inside markdown code blocks and support installation or API usage examples. No executable source file or automatic invocation was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The blocker findings for weak cryptographic algorithms are false positives. The cited lines describe AI vision functionality, prompts, parameters, or response examples, and no cryptographic operation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no hashing, cipher selection, or cryptographic API use on the cited lines. The matches appear to come from ordinary text and example payload content.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Dismissed
low_findings.description: The combined code execution, network, and credential heuristic is not confirmed as malicious. The files are documentation-only, the network destination is the stated verging.ai service, and no obfuscation or prompt injection attempt was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious factors coexist in curl examples, but the semantic context is normal API documentation. I did not find hidden instructions, encoded payloads, or automatic execution paths.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Calls to External Vision API
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on outbound calls to verging.ai endpoints for image URL analysis and file upload. This is intended functionality but should be disclosed before users submit sensitive images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both skill documentation and README examples show explicit HTTPS endpoints. The behavior is clear and central to the skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authorization Header in Example Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Example requests include API key Authorization headers. Placeholders are used, but real use requires users to provide a credential.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documentation shows placeholder keys and environment variable use. This confirms credential handling without evidence of embedded real secrets.

content.value_statement: Teams need quick image understanding without building a vision pipeline. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to call the verging.ai vision API for image descriptions, object lists, and text extraction.
actual_capabilities: Analyze an image from a public or accessible image URL.
Upload a local image file using multipart form data.
Use custom prompts to request descriptions, object detection, or text extraction.
Set max_tokens to control the response length.
Report token usage and credits consumed when the API returns them.
limitations: Requires a verging.ai API key and account credits.
Sends image content and prompts to the verging.ai service.
Does not include local image processing or offline analysis.
Depends on upstream GPT-4o Vision availability and rate limits.

use_cases.title: Catalog Product Photos
use_cases.description: Generate concise descriptions and visible attribute lists from product images.

use_cases.title: Extract Text From Screenshots
use_cases.description: Read visible text from screenshots and summarize the important details.

use_cases.title: Review Visual Evidence
use_cases.description: Ask targeted questions about objects, layout, and visible conditions in an image.

prompt_templates.title: Describe an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vision skill to describe this image: [image URL]. Focus on the main subjects, setting, and visible text.

prompt_templates.title: Find Objects
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this image file: [file path]. List the visible objects, their approximate positions, and any uncertainty.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Screenshot Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the vision API on this screenshot: [image URL]. Extract visible text and summarize the user interface state.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Against Criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this uploaded image against these criteria: [criteria]. Return observations, missing evidence, and confidence notes.

output_examples.input: A URL for a product photo with a prompt asking for listing copy.
output_examples.output: The response describes the product, visible materials, color, packaging, and any readable labels.

output_examples.input: A local screenshot with a prompt asking for visible text and interface state.
output: Visible text is extracted in reading order.
Unclear or partially hidden text is marked as uncertain.

output_examples.input: An uploaded inspection photo with a checklist prompt.
output_examples.output: The response lists matching checklist items, missing evidence, and areas that need human review.

best_practices: Use environment variables for API keys and avoid pasting real keys into prompts.
Do not submit images that contain sensitive data unless your policy allows third-party processing.
Write specific prompts that define the visual details you need.
anti_patterns: Do not use private internal image URLs unless they are approved for external access.
Do not treat visual analysis as proof when the image is blurry or incomplete.
Do not request broad descriptions when you need a precise checklist answer.

faq.question: Does this skill process images locally?
faq.answer: No. It sends image URLs or uploaded files to the verging.ai API.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: You need a verging.ai API key with enough account credits.

faq.question: Can it analyze local image files?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented upload endpoint supports multipart file uploads.

faq.question: Can it read text in screenshots?
faq.answer: Yes. You can prompt it to extract visible text, but unclear text may need review.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive images?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill sends images and prompts to a third-party API.
